user.title: Set up your Fire TV
user.description:
How to find your Fire TV IP address:
Settings → My Fire TV → About → Network → IP Address

How to enable ADB Debugging:
Settings → My Fire TV → Developer Options → ADB Debugging → ON

After clicking Submit, look at your TV screen, approve the USB debugging prompt within 30 seconds.

Both devices must be on the same Wi-Fi network.

data_description.host: Example: 192.168.1.100 (found at Settings → My Fire TV → About → Network)
data_description.port: Default is 5555, only change if you modified it
data_description.name: Friendly name shown on your dashboard

error.cannot_connect: Could not connect. Check the IP address is correct, ADB Debugging is enabled, and approve the connection prompt on your TV screen. You have 30 seconds to approve after clicking Submit.

abort.already_configured: This Fire TV is already configured.

init.title: Polling Settings
init.description: Control how often the integration checks your Fire TV for updates.

data_description.scan_interval: How often to check which app is running (lower = faster updates, higher CPU)
data_description.screenshot_interval: How often to capture a new screenshot of the screen

custom_apps.title: Custom App Names
custom_apps.description:
Rename apps that show incorrectly on your dashboard. Use the App Package sensor to find the package name, then add one entry per line.

Format: package.name = Display Name

Examples:
com.apple.atv = Apple TV
tv.twitch.android.viewer = Twitch

data_description.custom_apps_text: One per line: package.name = Display Name
